ddls:
CREATE TABLE "ports" (
  "port_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each port */,
  "port_name" TEXT /* Name or identifier of the port */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device to which the port belongs */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the port (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the port */,
  "speed" TEXT /* Speed of the port (e.g., 10 Gbps, 1 Gbps) */,
  "duplex" TEXT /* Duplex mode of the port (e.g., full-duplex, half-duplex) */,
  "mtu" INTEGER /* Maximum transmission unit (MTU) of the port */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("port_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ports_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name or identifier of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device (e.g., switch, router) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the device */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the device */,
  "subnet_mask" TEXT /* Subnet mask of the device */,
  "gateway" TEXT /* Gateway IP address of the device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "frame_types" (
  "frame_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each frame type */,
  "frame_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the frame type (e.g., good broadcast, pause) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the frame type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("frame_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "frame_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each frame statistic entry */,
  "port_id" INTEGER /* ID of the port where the frame was received */,
  "frame_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the frame type */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the statistic was recorded */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Count of frames of the specific type received on the port */,
  "bytes_transferred" INTEGER /* Total bytes transferred for the specific frame type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_frame_statistics_frame_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("frame_type_id") REFERENCES frame_types ("frame_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_frame_statistics_port_id FOREIGN KEY ("port_id") REFERENCES ports ("port_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_frames" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error frame entry */,
  "port_id" INTEGER /* ID of the port where the error frame was received */,
  "error_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the error type */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error was recorded */,
  "count" INTEGER /* Count of error frames of the specific type received on the port */,
  "error_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_frames_port_id FOREIGN KEY ("port_id") REFERENCES ports ("port_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_frames_error_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("error_type_id") REFERENCES error_types ("error_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_types" (
  "error_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error type */,
  "error_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the error type (e.g., invalid FCS, non-integral octets) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the error type */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the error (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, network engineer, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout, view report) */,
  "resource" TEXT /* Resource accessed (e.g., port statistics, error logs) */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration entry */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the configuration belongs to */,
  "config_text" TEXT /* Text of the device configuration */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of the configuration */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the configuration was applied */,
  "applied_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who applied the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_configurations_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alarms" (
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alarm event */,
  "port_id" INTEGER /* ID of the port that triggered the alarm */,
  "alarm_type" TEXT /* Type of alarm (e.g., high error rate, unusual traffic pattern) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alarm was triggered */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alarm */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alarm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarms_port_id FOREIGN KEY ("port_id") REFERENCES ports ("port_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alarm_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alarm configuration entry */,
  "port_id" INTEGER /* ID of the port the alarm configuration belongs to */,
  "alarm_type" TEXT /* Type of alarm (e.g., high error rate, unusual traffic pattern) */,
  "threshold" INTEGER /* Threshold value for the alarm */,
  "notification_email" TEXT /* Email address to send notifications to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alarm_configurations_port_id FOREIGN KEY ("port_id") REFERENCES ports ("port_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification event */,
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alarm that triggered the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the notification */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "recipient_email" TEXT /* Email address of the recipient */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_alarm_id FOREIGN KEY ("alarm_id") REFERENCES alarms ("alarm_id")
)
